From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1977 studio album by Willis Jackson

| In the Alley | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Studio album](/wiki/Album "Album") by [Willis Jackson](/wiki/Willis_Jackson_(saxophonist) "Willis Jackson (saxophonist)") | |
| Released | 1977 |
| Recorded | 1976 |
| Studio | Dimensional Sounds Studio, New York City |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | [Jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | [Muse](/wiki/Muse_Records "Muse Records") MR 5100 |
| [Producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") | [Fred Seibert](/wiki/Fred_Seibert "Fred Seibert") |
| [Willis Jackson](/wiki/Willis_Jackson_(saxophonist) "Willis Jackson (saxophonist)") chronology | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | *[Plays with Feeling](/w/index.php?title=Plays_with_Feeling&action=edit&redlink=1 "Plays with Feeling (page does not exist)")* (1976) | ***In the Alley*** (1977) | *[The Gator Horn](/wiki/The_Gator_Horn "The Gator Horn")* (1977) | | |

***In the Alley*** is an album by saxophonist [Willis Jackson](/wiki/Willis_Jackson_(saxophonist) "Willis Jackson (saxophonist)") which was recorded in 1976 and first released on the [Muse](/wiki/Muse_Records "Muse Records") label.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=In_the_Alley&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Reception")]

Professional ratings

| Review scores | |
| --- | --- |
| Source | Rating |
| [Allmusic](/wiki/Allmusic "Allmusic") | StarStarStar[[3]](#cite_note-Allmusic-3) |
| *[The Rolling Stone Jazz Record Guide](/wiki/The_Rolling_Stone_Jazz_Record_Guide "The Rolling Stone Jazz Record Guide")* | StarStarStar[[4]](#cite_note-RSJRG-4) |

In his review on [Allmusic](/wiki/Allmusic "Allmusic"), Scott Yanow calls the album a "typical but often exciting outing" and states "The music includes blues, romps, a ballad, and funky vamps".[[3]](#cite_note-Allmusic-3)

## Track listing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=In_the_Alley&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Track listing")]

All compositions by Willis Jackson except where noted.

1. "Niamani" (Sonny Phillips) – 7:25
2. "Gator's Groove" (Yusef Ali) – 7:07
3. "Blues Blues Blues" – 5:09
4. "Young Man With a Horn" ([Ray Anthony](/wiki/Ray_Anthony "Ray Anthony")) – 5:58
5. "[More](/wiki/More_(Theme_from_Mondo_Cane) "More (Theme from Mondo Cane)")" ([Nino Oliviero](/wiki/Nino_Oliviero "Nino Oliviero"), [Riz Ortolani](/wiki/Riz_Ortolani "Riz Ortolani"), [Marcello Ciorciolini](/wiki/Marcello_Ciorciolini "Marcello Ciorciolini"), [Norman Newell](/wiki/Norman_Newell "Norman Newell")) – 7:45
6. "In the Alley" – 9:14

## Personnel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=In_the_Alley&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personnel")]

* [Willis Jackson](/wiki/Willis_Jackson_(saxophonist) "Willis Jackson (saxophonist)") – [tenor saxophone](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone")
* [Sonny Phillips](/wiki/Sonny_Phillips "Sonny Phillips") – [piano](/wiki/Piano "Piano")
* Carl Wilson – [organ](/wiki/Electronic_organ "Electronic organ")
* [Jimmy Ponder](/wiki/Jimmy_Ponder "Jimmy Ponder") – [guitar](/wiki/Guitar "Guitar")
* [Jimmy Lewis](/wiki/Jimmy_Lewis_(bassist) "Jimmy Lewis (bassist)") – [bass](/wiki/Double_bass "Double bass")
* Yusef Ali – [drums](/wiki/Drum_kit "Drum kit")
* Buddy Caldwell – [congas](/wiki/Congas "Congas"), [percussion](/wiki/Percussion "Percussion")
